few: determiner. An indefinite, but usually small, number of.
fie: interjection. Sometimes followed by on or upon: used to express distaste, disgust, or outrage.
fir: noun. A conifer of the genus Abies.
fire: noun. A (usually self-sustaining) chemical reaction involving the bonding of oxygen with carbon or other fuel, with the production of heat
firer: noun. A person who fires a weapon; a shooter.
firework: noun. A device using gunpowder and other chemicals which, when lit, emits a combination of coloured flames, sparks, whistles or bangs, and
fore: adjective. Former; occurring earlier (in some order); previous.
fork: noun. Any of several types of pronged (tined) tools (physical tools), as follows: A utensil with spikes used to put solid food into the
forker: noun. An agricultural laborer who uses a fork.
forkier: adjective. comparative form of forky: more forky
fro: adverb. From; away; back or backward.
froe: noun. A cleaving tool for splitting cask staves and shingles from a block of wood.
frow: noun. A woman; a wife, especially a Dutch or German one.
kefir: noun. A fermented milk drink from the Caucasus and Eastern Europe, similar to yogurt but more liquid.
kerf: noun. The act of cutting or carving something; a stroke or slice.
kif: noun. A kind of cannabis smoked in Morocco and Algeria, for its narcotic or intoxicating effects.
kir: noun. A cocktail made with a measure of crème de cassis topped up with white wine.
koi: noun. Ornamental domesticated varieties of the Amur carp (Cyprinus rubrofuscus), of Japan and eastern Asia with red-gold or white coloring.
kor: noun. Alternative form of cor: a former Hebrew and Phoenician unit of volume.
kore: noun. An Ancient Greek statue of a woman, portrayed standing, usually clothed, painted in bright colours and having an elaborate hairstyle.
ore: noun. Rock or other material that contains valuable or utilitarian materials; primarily a rock containing metals or gems for which it is
owe: verb. To be under an obligation to give something back to someone or to perform some action for someone.
rework: noun. The act of redoing, correcting, or rebuilding.
rif: verb. Alternative letter-case form of RIF.
rife: adjective. Widespread, common, prevalent, current (mainly of unpleasant or harmful things).
rifer: adjective. comparative form of rife: more rife
row: noun. A line of objects, often regularly spaced, such as seats in a theatre, vegetable plants in a garden, etc.
weir: noun. An adjustable dam placed across a river to regulate the flow of water downstream.
wife: noun. A married woman, especially in relation to her spouse.
wire: noun. Metal formed into a thin, even thread, now usually by being drawn through a hole in a steel die.
wirer: noun. A tool to assist in installing wire.
woe: noun. Great sadness or distress; a misfortune causing such sadness.
wok: noun. A large, round-bottomed cooking pan used in East Asian cooking.
woke: adjective. Awake: conscious and not asleep.
worker: noun. One who works: a person who performs labor for a living; traditionally, especially, manual labor.
wrier: adjective. comparative form of wry: more wry
eir: determiner. Belonging to em, gender-neutral third-person singular possessive adjective, equivalent to the singular their and coordinate
erk: noun. A member of the groundcrew in the RAF.
firk: verb. To carry away or about; carry; move.
fow: verb. To clean, cleanse (out), as in cooking utensils or house maintenance.
fri: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Western Frisian.
frower: noun. A froe or frow (cleaving tool).
ike: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eastern Canadian Inuktitut.
iwo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iwur.
kie: noun. Alternative spelling of kye, plural of cow
koe: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kacipo-Balesi.
kori: noun. The monetary unit of Kutch prior to 1947, divided into 24 dokdas.
oie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Okolie.
oik: noun. a member of the lower classes.
oker: noun. Interest on money; usury; increase.
orf: noun. An exanthemous disease caused by a parapox virus, occurring primarily in sheep and goats but also capable of infecting humans.
orfe: noun. A fish, the ide, Leuciscus idus.
ower: noun. A person who owes something, especially money.
rew: noun. Obsolete or dialectal form of row
rio: noun. Alternative form of ryō (“Japanese ounce”).
roke: noun. Fog, mist; light rain; smoke, vapour; damp.
weki: noun. Alternative form of wheki (“the tree fern Dicksonia squarrosa”).
wer: noun. A man; a male; a husband.
wir: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Wiraféd.
